locations.name: NC ABC state warehouse
locations.notes: NC is fragmented by local ABC boards. Wake can expose store-level inventory, NC Stock Shipped Data gives board-level shipment evidence, and the NC warehouse page is statewide early-warning radar. Board/warehouse signals are useful planning intelligence but must not be described as exact shelf inventory.

locations.name: North Carolina ABC + county boards
locations.notes: NC is fragmented by local ABC boards. Wake can expose store-level inventory, NC Stock Shipped Data gives board-level shipment evidence, and the NC warehouse page is statewide early-warning radar. Board/warehouse signals are useful planning intelligence but must not be described as exact shelf inventory.

locations.notes: NC is fragmented by local ABC boards. Board/warehouse signals are useful planning intelligence but must not be described as exact shelf inventory.

locations.name: Ohio OHLQ
locations.notes: OHLQ store-level product availability is collected through browser/CDP via /api/product-availability/{sku} with RequestVerificationToken from the rendered page. The frontend bundle decodes hashed availability buckets as Not Available, Sold Out, Limited Supply, and In Stock. Direct server fetch is Cloudflare-gated and OHLQ does not expose explicit bottle counts, so alerts should describe status, not quantity.
